title: Integrate ML Design with cross-module bridge system
description:
ML Design module has zero BridgeLink components despite the bridge system existing. Add bridge links for:
- Feature Store → Database module (Redis/S3 architecture)
- Pipeline Builder → System Design module (Kafka/Spark infrastructure)
- A/B Testing → Algorithms module (statistical testing)
- Neural Network → Data Structures module (matrix operations)
- Model Serving → System Design module (load balancing, caching)
- Bandits → Algorithms module (online learning, optimization)

Also add ML Design concepts to concept-module-map.ts (currently only 2 entries: feature-store and model-serving).
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 BridgeLink instances across ML Design modes
concept-module-map.ts updated with 6+ ML concepts
Clicking a bridge link navigates to the target module with context
RecommendedBridges panel shows relevant cross-module suggestions
Bridge panel accessible from ML Design sidebar

title: Connect ML Design to progress-store for XP and mastery tracking
description:
progress-store.ts has ChallengeAttempt tracking with XP, streaks, and scores. cross-module-store.ts tracks per-module mastery. ML Design uses neither.

Integrate:
- Award XP when user completes a training run (10 XP)
- Award XP when user switches modes (5 XP for exploring)
- Track concept completion: mark 'neural-network' as visited, 'pipeline-builder' as visited, etc.
- Update module mastery in cross-module-store: theory (from watching simulations) + practice (from future challenges)
- Show mastery in sidebar header: 'ML Design — 2/6 modes explored'

This connects ML Design to the platform-wide progress system.
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting each mode updates cross-module-store conceptProgress
XP awarded for training completion, mode exploration
Module mastery updated in cross-module-store
Progress visible in sidebar header
SkillRadarChart shows ML Design mastery alongside other modules

title: Add URL deep linking for ML Design modes and topics
description:
The app runs as a SPA at '/' with no URL routing per module/topic. For ML Design, add URL support:
- /ml-design → opens ML Playground
- /ml-design?mode=neural-network → opens NN mode
- /ml-design?mode=ab-testing → opens A/B Testing mode
- /ml-design?concept=feature-store → opens Feature Store (bridge system compatible)

Use URL search params (not full routing) to avoid breaking the SPA architecture. Parse params on module load and set initial state accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: URL params set initial mode when navigating to ML Design
Sharing a URL with ?mode=pipeline-builder opens that mode directly
Browser back/forward works for mode changes
Bridge system paths (/ml-design?concept=feature-store) work
URL updates when user switches modes (without full page reload)

title: Add ML Design concepts to command palette search
description:
The command palette (⌘K) searches modules and commands but not individual concepts within modules. Add ML Design concepts as searchable items:
- 'Neural Network Training' → switches to ML Design, NN mode
- 'A/B Testing' → switches to ML Design, A/B Testing mode
- 'Feature Store' → switches to ML Design, Feature Store mode
- 'Multi-Armed Bandit' → switches to ML Design, Experimentation mode
- 'Pipeline Builder' → switches to ML Design, Pipeline mode
- 'Model Serving' → switches to ML Design, Serving mode

This makes every ML concept discoverable via unified search.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing 'neural network' in command palette shows ML Design NN mode
Typing 'bandit' shows Experimentation mode
Typing 'A/B test' shows A/B Testing mode
Selecting a result navigates to ML Design and sets the correct mode
At least 6 ML concepts searchable in command palette

title: Extend onboarding overlay to introduce ML Design for first-time visitors
description:
The onboarding overlay (onboarding-overlay.tsx) has 5 steps focused on System Design (drag components, connect nodes, run simulation). When a user visits ML Design for the first time, there should be a module-specific onboarding:
- Step 1: 'Welcome to ML Playground — train neural networks, simulate A/B tests, and explore ML systems.'
- Step 2: 'Choose a mode from these tabs' (spotlight mode tabs)
- Step 3: 'Adjust parameters and click Train' (spotlight NN sidebar)
- Step 4: 'Watch the decision boundary form' (spotlight canvas)

Trigger on first visit to ML Design (localStorage flag).
acceptanceCriteria: ML-Design-specific onboarding triggers on first visit
4 steps covering: intro, mode tabs, parameters, run
Spotlight highlights target elements correctly
Dismissible at any step
Not shown on subsequent visits (localStorage flag)
Re-accessible via sidebar '?' button

title: Add 'Continue where you left off' for ML Design on return visits
description:
When a user returns to ML Design after visiting another module, restore their last state:
- Last active mode (e.g., if they were in A/B Testing, return to A/B Testing)
- Last hyperparameter values (NN layers, neurons, learning rate)
- Last training results (if trained, show the result — don't require re-training)

Use localStorage via a dedicated ml-design-store (or extend ui-store partialize).
acceptanceCriteria: Returning to ML Design shows the last active mode
NN hyperparameters restored from last session
Trained network results shown if previously trained
Works across page refreshes and module switches
Graceful handling of corrupted localStorage (reset to defaults)
